cue: an operation adds a fixed increment pattern at a fixed set of relative cells (a stencil, e.g. +1,-2,+1), identical everywhere on a board or cycle, and you must decide reachability
note: all annihilate the stencil with a position-weighting/coloring to get a conserved quantity

cue: a functional equation or inequality in two free variables where setting one variable to f (or an iterate f^k) of the other makes terms recombine
note: the substitution collapses the relation into one among consecutive iterates of f

cue: a relation given for every index n, built from a running sum or a sliding-window product, so consecutive instances overlap in all but their end terms
note: subtract or divide two index-shifted copies; the shared block cancels to a per-index relation

cue: a quantity provably ranges over a finite state set (parity-vectors, response maps, residues, bounded prefix sums) while you collect more items than there are states
note: more collected items than available states forces a state to repeat

cue: the objects in play (a pair, a represented value, the differences of a sequence) share a common factor, or the minimal/extremal value is divisible
note: divide the common factor out to pass to a smaller or coprime equivalent instance (descent)

cue: a line is tangent to a circle at a named point, given as hypothesis or required as the goal
note: tangent-chord angle equals the inscribed angle on that chord, and its converse

cue: two figures have respectively parallel corresponding sides, or two triangles share their circumcenter and orthocenter
note: parallel correspondence forces a homothety (or translation); the task is to locate its center

cue: build or forbid a selection under a spacing/adjacency constraint, where each chosen element can be charged to a forced exclusion
note: amortize each included pick against a forced skip to bound the selection size

cue: a process carries a bounded, monotone integer count or convex potential whose target is exactly a small extreme value of it
note: classify which moves keep it from changing, forcing it to reach the target or halt

cue: a derived point lies on the extension of a side through a vertex, so an apex angle is replaced by its supplement
note: the supplement manufactures the second equal-angle pair needed for AA similarity

cue: a quantity is trapped between two bounds that coincide, or k copies each at least m sum to exactly k*m
note: coinciding bounds force equality throughout the chain

cue: an optimizer of a weighted quantity exists and a one-step-perturbed candidate is still admissible
note: read the governing inequality straight off the admissible perturbation

cue: an inequality carries a convex or exponential term r^t to be compared against a linear quantity
note: a tangent-line / Bernoulli bound linearizes the exponential contribution

cue: two divisibility relations share a common left factor
note: subtract one from a multiple of the other to collapse onto a tighter single divisibility

cue: two circles meet at a second point, or two triangles share a vertex with proportional / reflected sides
note: that common point is the spiral-similarity center carrying one chord/triangle to the other

cue: a point on a circle is equidistant from two others (equal chords), or is the second meeting of an angle bisector with the circumcircle
note: such a point is an arc-midpoint / circumcenter, giving a perpendicular, antiparallel chord, or central=2*inscribed

cue: an equation forces one side to be a pure prime power (or a divisibility by one) and the exponent is the unknown
note: extract the prime-exponent on each side and compare (LTE/Legendre downstream)

cue: a moving point keeps a constant difference, or a constant ratio, of distances to two fixed points or lines
note: constant difference yields a conic with those foci; constant ratio yields an Apollonius circle / fixed line

cue: a point is defined only as an intersection, and you must prove it also satisfies a separate positional property
note: introduce a phantom point carrying the wanted property and show it meets the defining condition, so they coincide

cue: three circles where each pair shares a center of similitude (mutually tangent or dilation-related), and the goal forces a fourth such center onto a known line or vertex
note: all reduce a concurrency/tangency goal to collinearity of the pairwise homothety centers of a circle-triple (Monge)

cue: a point at which two products of signed collinear segments are equal, or that has equal power to two circles (often read off from given equal segments or parallels)
note: all open by placing an equal-power point on a radical axis, yielding concyclic / coaxial / radical-center conclusions

cue: a symmetric expression pairing a set's sum with its reciprocal-sum, or a two-term sum whose two summands multiply to a quantity already controlled
note: expanding the cross terms of (sum)(reciprocal-sum) regroups them into the controlled diagonal products

cue: a product of positive quantities whose total sum is fixed, to be maximized or bounded
note: split the fixed sum into equal / complementary parts to extremize the product

cue: an extremal construction required for every n whose target size or bound is logarithmic (~log2 n) or ~2^k
note: build index-layers of geometrically doubling size (2-adic valuation / floor(log2)) so a monotone path or class meets at most one per layer

cue: a merge operation fuses two equal-size piles into one of double size, with a target pile count to reach
note: doubling-tournament ladder plus a reservoir pile; demoted from DOUBLING_CONSTRUCT because its trigger is a pile-merge op, not a log-bound layout

cue: a target value must be represented using a fixed prescribed number of distinct summands
note: pad to the required count with value-canceling (sign-symmetric) pairs that contribute zero to the total

cue: a prescribed structured multiset of pairwise differences (e.g. consecutive powers) must be realized by points on a line
note: place points as partial sums of powers of a root of a tailored recurrence so block-sums collapse to single powers

cue: a generating process closed under both addition and coordinatewise maximum, asked to reach a target tuple or bound the reachable count
note: build spike/basis vectors from generators spanning {1,index,index^2}; folds in the bound-side closure invariant for the same problem

cue: must construct a finite integer set in which every pairwise difference divides both of its endpoints
note: a fixed gadget {0,6,8,9,12} scaled by the lcm of its differences gives an infinite family

cue: must arrange a self-similar multiset (size-N built from copies of size-N/2) to a constant per-column or per-block target
note: recursive split T_m = (T_{m-1}+1) union {1..m}, two copies concatenated

cue: a prescribed per-row or per-position shift must be realized by cutting a strip into complementary-length blocks and transposing them
note: pieces sized n, n-1, ... paired with 1, 2, ... each summing to n, swap each pair

cue: must realize every prescribed value of a summary quantity (a for-all-targets attainment) from one template carrying a free scaling parameter
note: family (l, kl, kl, k^2 l) with l tuned to hit every non-squarefree total
